title: Nando's-Style Peri Peri Chicken
tagline: Homemade peri peri sauce, air-fried chicken and herby wedges — the whole Nando's plate.
servingsNote: Sauce makes ~200-250 ml — enough for this plus 2-3 more chicken meals in the fridge.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (500 g boneless chicken thigh + wedges from ~300 g potato + a portion of the sauce). Thigh is what drives the fat here.
Leaner version: use chicken breast instead of thigh — drops it to roughly ~480 kcal / 52 g protein per serving with far less fat. Air-frying (vs pan or deep fry) already keeps oil minimal.
The wedges are the carb load. Air-fry them with just 1-2 tsp oil, and if you're cutting, halve the potato and lean on the chicken.
The peri peri sauce itself is negligible calories per serving — most of the oil in the batch stays in the jar.

items: 2 red bell peppers (capsicum) — use only 1 for a stronger chilli-forward sauce
8-10 dried Kashmiri/Byadgi red chillies, soaked in hot water 15-20 min (or 6-8 fresh red chillies)
10-12 garlic cloves (blend most cooked, keep 2-3 raw for punch)
1 tbsp smoked paprika (or Kashmiri chilli powder + tiny pinch garam masala)
1 tbsp sugar (or 1 tbsp honey — pick one)
500 g chicken — thigh (juicier) or breast (leaner), boneless; or bone-in if you prefer
2 large potatoes (~300 g), cut into even wedges

steps.title: Roast the sauce base
steps.body: Char the 2 capsicums, onion and (fresh) chillies on an open flame or hot pan until the skins blister and blacken in spots. This char is what gives the smoky Nando's note — don't skip it. If using dried chillies, soak them in hot water 15-20 min first so they blend smooth, don't blend them dry.

steps.title: Cook the base
steps.body: Simmer the roasted capsicum + onion + chillies + 8-9 garlic cloves + 80 ml vinegar + lemon juice and zest + 1 tbsp smoked paprika + 3/4 tsp oregano + 1/2 tsp pepper + 1 tbsp sugar + 1 bay leaf + 1-1.5 tsp salt for 10-15 minutes until slightly thickened. Longer simmer = better keeping quality.

steps.title: Blend and emulsify
steps.body: Remove the bay leaf. Blend everything smooth, drizzling in the 60-80 ml oil while blending so it emulsifies. Add the 2-3 raw garlic cloves at the very end for a sharp top note. Taste: it should read acid > garlic > chilli, with capsicum as background body, NOT the star.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
steps.body: Coat the 500 g chicken with 4-5 tbsp of the sauce + 1/2 tsp salt. Marinate at least 30 minutes, ideally 2-4 hours (or overnight — the sauce also tastes far better the next day once the flavours integrate). Bottle the rest of the sauce for later.

steps.title: Prep the wedges
steps.body: Cut 2 potatoes (~300 g) into even wedges. For the no-boil route, soak them in water 15-20 min to pull out starch, then dry completely. (For crispier/fluffier, parboil 5-6 min instead, drain and dry.) Toss with 1-2 tsp oil, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp oregano and a pinch of cornflour. Dry potatoes are the key to crisp — wet ones just steam.

steps.title: Air-fry the chicken
steps.body: Shake excess marinade off the chicken (dripping sauce burns and turns bitter — light coating only). Air-fry at 180C for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway, then bump to 200C for a final 3-5 minutes for colour. Boneless ~15-18 min total, bone-in ~20-25 min. Brush a little fresh sauce on in the last 2-3 minutes for flavour without burning the marinade.

steps.title: Air-fry the wedges
steps.body: Air-fry the wedges at 200C for 18-22 minutes (or ~15-18 if parboiled), shaking halfway. Don't crowd the basket or they steam instead of crisping.

steps.title: Finish and plate
steps.body: Toss the hot wedges with a spoon of peri peri sauce + a little chopped fresh garlic and a squeeze of lemon. Squeeze lemon over the chicken too and sprinkle a little salt + chilli flakes — that final acid instantly rebalances everything.

notes: If the batch comes out too capsicumy/peppery — that green shimla-mirch smell is the classic peri peri failure. Cause: too much capsicum vs acid + garlic + chilli. Fix a finished batch by blending in 1-2 tbsp more vinegar + 1/2 tbsp lemon + 2-3 raw garlic cloves + 1/2 tsp salt + a pinch of sugar; add more soaked-chilli paste or paprika if it's still capsicum-heavy. A hacky rescue is 1 tsp ketchup (sweet + acid + body masks the capsicum).
Next-batch prevention: use just 1 capsicum, roast it until properly charred, and lean harder on chilli + vinegar + garlic. Target ratio in the mouth is acid > garlic > chilli > oil > capsicum.

Always judge peri peri the NEXT day — fresh it smells harsh and disjointed; after 12-24 hours in the fridge the flavours settle and the capsicum note drops. Cooking it onto chicken also mellows it a lot.
Storage: fridge 2-3 months (high vinegar + cooked base + oil layer on top + a sterilised jar), freezer 4-6 months in small portions.
This slots into a weekend batch-cook system: peri peri sauce + a dry rub + a yogurt/tandoori marinade covers a week of easy chicken.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
